person.name: Henry FitzHugh
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Father Sir Henry FitzHugh, Constable of Barnard Castle d. 1356

Mother Eve de Bulmer d. b 25 Nov 1337

Sir Henry FitzHenry, Lord FitzHugh was lord of Ravensworth, Richmondshire, Yorkshire, England. 

He became heir of his father because his older brother Hugh predeceased him, having died before 15 February 1332/3 (1333).

He married Joan de Fourneux, daughter of Sir Richard de Fourneux and Sibella, between March 1330 and September 1331.

They had 1 son (Henry, 2nd Lord FitzHugh) & 1 daughter (Joan, wife of William Greystoke, 2nd Lord Greystoke, Lord FitzWilliam, of Sir Anthony de Lucy, 3rd Lord Lucy, & of Sir Matthew Redman).

Sir Henry FitzHenry, Lord FitzHugh died on 24 September 1352. He was buried in Jervaulx Abbey, Yorkshire.

«b»Family«/b»
Joan de Fourneux d. 21 Sep 1349. She was an heiress of her brother William.

«b»Children«/b»
1.) Hugh fitz Henry. He died without issue during the life of his father, before 21 September 1349. He had married Isabel Neville.

2.) Joan fitz Henry. d. 1 Sep 1403

3.) Henry fitz Hugh. 2nd Lord FitzHugh of Ravensworth. Born about 1338 (15 in 1353), d. 29 Aug 1386.

In 5 Edward III [25 January 1331-24 January 1332], at Chester, Henry Fitzhenry and Joan his wife, late wife of John de Orreby, sued for dower:

Roger son of Robert le Bruyn, of Stapelford, eight acres of pasture, six acres of wood, and three acres of moor in Foukestapelford;
John son of Nicholas de Huxlegh, three acres and a half of meadow and two acres of moor in Foukestapelford;
Robert son of William de Huxlegh, one messuage and eight acres of land in Foukestapelford;
John son of William Cocus, three messuages and fifteen acres of land in Foukestapelford;
William son of William de Rosmegrene, one messuage and six and a half acres of land Foukestapelford;
William de Wetfeld, five messuages and twelve acres of land in Foukestapelford; and
Ellen who was the wife of Richard de Fouleshurst, three messuages, one water-mill, fifty-six acres of land, six acres of meadow, and three acres of wood in Wich-Malbank and Batynton.

The said Roger, John, Robert, John, William, William, and Ellen vouch to warranty John son of Philip de Orreby kinsman and heir of John de Orreby.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Henry FitzHugh於1292年出生在英國越可爾拉文斯沃斯時，他的父親Henry FitzHugh26歲，母親Eve de Bulmer18歲。他於大約1324年與Joan Fourneux在英國越可爾拉文斯沃斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1352年9月24日在他的家鄉享年60歲，埋葬在英國越可爾易亞斯特 維頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Henry FitzHugh于1292年出生在英国越可爾拉文斯沃斯时，他的父亲Henry FitzHugh26岁，母亲Eve de Bulmer18岁。他于大约1324年与Joan Fourneux在英国越可爾拉文斯沃斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1352年9月24日在他的家乡享年60岁，埋葬在英国越可爾易亞斯特 維頓。

nameMeanings.name: FitzHugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Henry FitzHugh was born in 1292, in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England, his father, Henry FitzHugh, was 26 and his mother, Eve de Bulmer, was 18. He married Joan Fourneux about 1324, in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 24 September 1352, in his hometown, at the age of 60, and was buried in East Witton, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Henry FitzHugh est né en 1292, à Ravensworth, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Henry FitzHugh, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Eve de Bulmer, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Joan Fourneux environ 1324, à Ravensworth, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 24 septembre 1352, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterré à East Witton, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Henry FitzHugh 1292 in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry FitzHugh 26 und seine Mutter Eve de Bulmer 18. Er hat um 1324 in Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England Joan Fourneux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 24. September 1352 im Alter von 60 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in East Witton, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Henry FitzHugh è nato il nel 1292 nel Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Henry FitzHugh, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Eve de Bulmer, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Joan Fourneux circa 1324, nel Ravensworth, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 24 settembre 1352, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 60 anni, e fu sepolto a East Witton, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry FitzHughが1292年にイギリスヨークシュアイアラベンスウオルスで生まれた時，父Henry FitzHughは26歳，母Eve de Bulmerは18歳であった。約1324年にイギリスヨークシュアイアラベンスウオルスでJoan Fourneuxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1352年9月24日に故郷で60歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアエアスト ウィットオンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry FitzHugh(이)가 1292년 영국 요르크실르 레이븐스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry FitzHugh(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Eve de Bulmer(은)는 18세였다. 그는 대략 1324년 영국 요르크실르 레이븐스워스에서 Joan Fourneux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1352년 9월 24일 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 이스트 윗튼에 묻혔다.

mn.Ravensworth, North Riding, Yorkshire, En: Ravensworth, Eoferwic, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Henry FitzHugh nasceu em 1292, em Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry FitzHugh, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Eve de Bulmer, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Fourneux aproximadamente 1324, em Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 24 de setembro de 1352, em sua cidade natal, com 60 anos, e foi sepultado em East Witton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Henry FitzHugh родился в 1292 г. в местности равенсворт, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry FitzHugh было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Eve de Bulmer, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Fourneux около 1324 г. в местности равенсворт, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 24 сентября 1352 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет и был похоронен в местности эаст виттон, еркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Henry FitzHugh nació en 1292, en Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry FitzHugh, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Eve de Bulmer, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Joan Fourneux aproximadamente 1324, en Ravensworth, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 24 de septiembre de 1352, en su pueblo, a la edad de 60 años, y fue sepultado en East Witton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.
